old_claim.note: Technical Drawing Standards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Arsenale's drydocks, wet docks, workshops, and sequential work sheds supplied the physical shipyard system in which stored subparts could be assembled and ships floated between production stages.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening and Shipyard & Workshops sections describe the drydocks, wet docks, workshops, sequential sheds, stored subparts, and ships floated between assembly stages.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The reviewed history presents prefabricated-part production as an operation of the Arsenale's integrated shipyard infrastructure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Docks and sequential work sheds are physical components of the documented production system; the removed drawing node is later and concerns another shipbuilding tradition.

ontology_before: Venetian prefabrication inherited from late technical drawing despite no direct Venetian evidence and an incompatible date.
ontology_after: Venetian prefabrication inherits from the shipyard and dock infrastructure in which its production sequence physically operated.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: shipyard_drydocks replaces technical_drawing_standards as the direct enabling prerequisite.
would_reject_if: Primary production records showed that the 1574 prefabrication system depended on the later scoped drawing convention but not on the Arsenale's docks and sequential work sheds.
short_reason: The source directly documents shipyard infrastructure as the production system.
